slides.title: Emotions, Behaviour, and Identity
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 4 English, Unit 7: Emotions, Behaviour, and Identity. In this unit you will learn how to talk about emotions (how we feel), behaviour (how we act), and identity (who we are and where our family comes from). You will read about children who feel nervous, curious, proud, and shy — and who learn to be gentle, polite, and generous to others. You will practise saying how you feel, giving kind advice, and describing what makes your own family special. By the end, you will be ready to understand this unit's story, “The Day of the Play,” in which a girl called Nadia finds her courage on stage. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Name many feelings and describe them, using words like nervous, anxious, proud, and curious
Describe how people behave, using words like gentle, selfish, generous, and polite
Talk about experiences in your life using the present perfect (“I have felt nervous before.”)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to name many feelings and describe them, using words like nervous, anxious, proud, and curious. you will also describe how people behave, using words like gentle, selfish, generous, and polite. you will also talk about experiences in your life using the present perfect (“i have felt nervous before.”). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: nervous, anxious, terrified, doubtful, curious, proud. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes The Day Before the Test and Where My Family Comes From. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Present Perfect (for life experiences)
bullets: We use the present perfect to talk about things we have done or felt at some time in our life, when the exact time does not matter. It connects the past...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Present Perfect (for life experiences). We use the present perfect to talk about things we have done or felt at some time in our life, when the exact time does not matter. It connects the past to now. Saying “I have felt nervous before” shows the feeling happened, and it still helps us understand ourselves today. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: How Do You Feel?. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
